reasoning: As of mid-May 2025, starlink's launch in Sri Lanka has been explicitly stalled by President Dissanayake over national security and data-access concerns. Although the telecom regulator (TRCSL) originally anticipated an April 2025 launch, the President's public announcement in early May citing "no legal grounds to access the system" and dissatisfaction with Starlink's proposed data dashboard indicates a major renegotiation of the initial agreement is required. With the resolution deadline on July 1, 2025 (only 45 days after the knowledge cutoff), there is very little time for the Sri Lankan government and Starlink to resolve the legal, technical, and political hurdles for lawful interception, implement the system, and officially launch the service. Therefore, resolution before July 2025 is highly unlikely.
